hypothesis: If a Transformer model with patch-based spectrum embeddings is pretrained on large simulated IR spectra and fine-tuned on real spectra while jointly optimizing a spectral-reconstruction alignment head across multiple concentration ratios, then it will more accurately identify constituent molecular structures from binary and ternary mixture IR spectra—including experimental ATR-IR data—than models without pretraining or the alignment head, because the reconstruction constraint enforces disentangled pure-component representations that generalize across mixture compositions.
